title: plantago major subsp. sinuata
intro: Plantago major subsp. sinuata, also known as greater plantain, is a subspecies of the common plantain plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is a perennial herb with broad leaves and is commonly found in lawns, meadows, and along roadsides. The leaves and seeds of the plant have been used for centuries in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of greater plantain include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and aiding in wound healing, and it can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and topical creams.
